verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B is canonically a soluble mitochondrial matrix protein (F1 catalytic core). However, literature documents ectopic translocation to the cell surface under non-baseline conditions: hepatocytes under hypoxia/acidic (tumor-like) conditions, endothelial cells during disturbed flow and hypercholesterolemia, cancer-cell glycolytic switching, and cholesterol-exposure-induced caveolar display. The baseline state is mitochondrial-internal; the surface pool is cell-state-induced.
